title: Longevity Might Be Biopharma's Next Big Thing
description: Longevity startups attracted $8.5B in VC in 2024. Major pharma companies are building entire divisions around aging biology, driven by GLP-1 success and AI-powered drug discovery.

title: Klothea Launches Phase 1b Human Trial for AKL003 mRNA Longevity Therapy
description: Klothea has begun a phase 1b human trial for AKL003, an mRNA therapy boosting alpha-Klotho protein levels. The trial in Honduras measures epigenetic clocks, inflammatory markers, and quality of life.

title: FDA Ends 'Two Trial Dogma' -- One Pivotal Trial May Now Suffice for Drug Approval
description: The FDA officially ended the requirement for two pivotal trials, allowing one rigorous trial for drug approval. This could shave 3-5 years off development timelines for aging-targeted therapies.

title: Multi-Ingredient Longevity Supplement Improves Vascular Aging Markers
description: A clinical trial of NOVOS Core (12 bioactive compounds) showed improved endothelial function, arterial flexibility, and systolic blood pressure over six months compared to placebo.

title: The 10 Breakthroughs That Changed Longevity in 2025
description: Sleep emerged as a stronger longevity predictor than diet or exercise. AI screened 1.1B compounds for aging drugs. Combination therapies outperformed single interventions across multiple studies.

title: Multi-Scale Longevity: Defeating Aging from Cells to Embodied Human Minds
description: New preprint by Michael Levin framing aging within collective intelligence of cells. An invited chapter for Springer Nature's 'Frontiers of Longevity Science' exploring multi-scale longevity approaches.

title: Blocking IL-11 Makes Mice Live Healthier and Longer
description: Study shows blocking the pro-inflammatory cytokine IL-11 (by deletion or therapeutic antibody) extends mouse healthspan and lifespan, with visible rejuvenation effects.

title: Therapeutic Plasma Exchange Reduces Biological Age by 2.6 Years
description: First placebo-controlled trial shows TPE combined with IVIG reduced biological age by 2.6 years via multi-omics biomarkers, reversing age-related immune decline.

title: Rapamycin for Longevity: Pros, Cons, and Future Perspectives
description: Comprehensive review covering PEARL trial results showing low-dose intermittent rapamycin was well-tolerated over 1 year with modest biomarker changes. Long-term clinical benefits remain to be established.

title: Top 10 Longevity & Anti-Aging Breakthroughs of 2025
description: ITP's 20-year review confirms rapamycin as the most reliable lifespan extender. Rapamycin plus acarbose showed up to 36.6% median lifespan increase. GLP-1s proposed as first true longevity medications.

title: Novartis-BioAge Collaboration Worth Up to $550M for Aging-Exercise Biology
description: Novartis partnered with BioAge Labs ($20M upfront, up to $550M) to discover drug targets at the intersection of aging and exercise biology using human longevity datasets.

title: ARPA-H Launches PROSPR Program to Extend Healthspan by 20 Years
description: ARPA-H committed up to $52M to Buck Institute's PATH project, recruiting people 50+ for advanced aging analytics. Extending healthspan by 1 year in 10% of the aging population could save $29B annually.

title: Insilico Medicine Unveils Oral GLP-1 Small Molecule Longevity Portfolio
description: Insilico Medicine revealed two unique oral GLP-1 small molecule drugs (QD and QW dosing) plus oral NLRP3 and APJ candidates targeting cardiometabolism and longevity.

title: Longevity Biotech in 2025: The Expert Roundup
description: Five leading experts reflect on 2025: GLP-1s validated longevity beyond weight loss, big pharma embraced aging biology, and partial reprogramming advanced toward human trials in 2026.

title: Longevity Biotech Fellowship Announces Cohort 6 in SF Bay Area
description: LBF Cohort 6 launches with a kickoff retreat in Berkeley, March 31 - April 4. Over 700 members work on solving aging through biostasis, replacement, and bioengineering approaches.

title: Lifting Weights Reduces Brain Age by Up to 2.3 Years
description: One year of strength training reduced older adults' estimated brain age by 1.4-2.3 years and enhanced functional brain connectivity, with effects persisting at 2-year follow-up.

title: Heat Exposure as Antidepressant: Sauna Effects Last 6-8 Weeks
description: A single session raising core body temperature by 2.6-3.7F produced antidepressant effects lasting 6-8 weeks. Combined with CBT, improvements reached ~16 points on depression scales.

title: Panaxatriol Discovered as Novel Senolytic; Voclosporin Extends C. elegans Lifespan
description: AI-based senolytic predictor identified panaxatriol as a novel senolytic agent. Voclosporin extended C. elegans lifespan by 19.1%, outperforming metformin's 11.3%.

title: SGLT2 Inhibitors May Lengthen Telomeres in Humans
description: Henagliflozin lengthened telomeres in 90.5% of participants after 26 weeks vs. 65.6% for placebo. SGLT2 inhibitors also showed senolytic properties and extended mouse lifespan by 14%.

title: ER-Phagy Identified as Novel Druggable Aging Pathway
description: Study in Science found dramatic reduction in rough ER with age via ER-phagy in C. elegans. This structural remodeling presents a new therapeutic target for age-related conditions.

title: The $63 Billion Longevity Revolution: Market Projected to Triple by 2035
description: Global longevity market valued at $21.3B in 2024 is projected to reach $63B by 2035 (10.37% CAGR). Driven by cellular reprogramming, senolytics, AI drug discovery, and demographic shifts.

title: Rapamycin Shows Limited Evidence for Longevity in Healthy Adults
description: Systematic review found no clear clinical evidence that low-dose rapamycin extends healthspan in healthy humans. Off-label use growing but long-term safety unknown. Larger trials urgently needed.

title: Telomere Science and Age Reversal: 5 Real Innovations for 2026
description: Viral thread (350+ reposts, 120K impressions) covering top 2026 longevity breakthroughs including telomere-related anti-aging trials and hyperbaric oxygen therapy for biological age reversal.

title: Human Longevity Is ~50% Heritable, Not 25% as Previously Thought
description: Study in Science using Danish/Swedish twin cohorts shows intrinsic lifespan heritability is 50-55%. Dementia had highest heritability (~0.7 by age 80). Genetics play a larger role in longevity than believed.

title: Calico VP Cynthia Kenyon Reflects on Redefining Aging Research
description: In TIME's new Longevity series, Calico VP of Aging Research Cynthia Kenyon discusses how foundational research on aging mechanisms continues to guide efforts toward real-world human health impact.
